encounter.reason: Interim restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis for non-small cell lung cancer on clinical trial
encounter.service_provider: The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center

conditions.name: non-small cell lung cancer, stage IIIA, adenocarcinoma, right upper lobe

medications.name: pembrolizumab 200 mg intravenous infusion
medications.reason: non-small cell lung cancer (maintenance therapy per clinical trial protocol)

medications.name: carboplatin AUC 5 intravenous
medications.reason: non-small cell lung cancer (induction chemotherapy, completed 4 cycles per protocol)

medications.name: pemetrexed 500 mg/m² intravenous
medications.reason: non-small cell lung cancer (induction chemotherapy, completed 4 cycles per protocol)

medications.name: gabapentin 300 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 300 mg by mouth at bedtime for neuropathy

medications.name: folic acid 1 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 1 mg by mouth once daily

medications.name: cyanocobalamin 1000 mcg intramuscular injection
medications.sig: 1000 mcg intramuscular injection every 9 weeks per supplementation protocol

document_references.description: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with IV contrast for interim restaging of stage IIIA NSCLC. Right upper lobe mass decreased from 4.2 cm to 2.8 cm (33% reduction), decreased mediastinal lymphadenopathy, no new lesions or distant metastases. RECIST 1.1 assessment: partial response.

diagnostic_reports.description: Interim restaging CT for stage IIIA non-small cell lung cancer performed with IV contrast. RECIST 1.1 assessment shows partial response — right upper lobe target lesion decreased from 4.2 cm to 2.8 cm (33% reduction from baseline, meeting ≥30% threshold). Right paratracheal (station 4R) node decreased from 2.1 cm to 1.2 cm; subcarinal (station 7) node decreased from 1.5 cm to 0.8 cm. No new pulmonary nodules, no pleural effusion, no hepatic lesions, normal adrenals, no osseous metastases. Comparison to baseline CT September 2024.
